title: ToV Proofs — Harm Attenuation Theorem (T10.2)
description: The Attenuation Theorem: under the Markov assumption, harm probability decreases exponentially with protective depth. Compute harm propagation, attenuation rates, protective depth requirements, and verify the attenuation property. Pure Rust via nexcore-tov-proofs.

tools.name: harm-probability
tools.description: Compute combined harm probability from a chain of propagation probabilities. Under Axiom 5 (Markov), ℙ(H|δs₁) = ∏ᵢP_{i→i+1}. Each probability must be in (0, 1).

parameters.name: probabilities
parameters.description: Array of propagation probabilities, each in (0, 1). Example: [0.5, 0.3, 0.2]

tools.name: exponential-harm
tools.description: Compute harm probability using exponential decay formula ℙ(H) = e^{-α(H-1)} where α is the attenuation rate and H is the harm level (depth).

parameters.name: harm_level
parameters.description: Harm level H (depth in the propagation chain, ≥ 1)

tools.name: protective-depth
tools.description: Compute minimum protective depth H such that ℙ(H) < target_probability. Formula: H ≥ 1 + log(1/ε)/α. Answers: 'how many safety layers do I need?'

parameters.name: target_probability
parameters.description: Target probability threshold ε, in (0, 1). Example: 0.05 for 5% residual risk

tools.name: verify-attenuation
tools.description: Verify the attenuation property holds: harm probability strictly decreases with each additional protective layer. Returns true if ℙ(H₁) > ℙ(H₂) > ... > ℙ(Hₙ).

parameters.name: probabilities
parameters.description: Array of propagation probabilities, each in (0, 1)

tools.name: attenuation-rate
tools.description: Compute the attenuation rate α = -log(P̄) where P̄ is the geometric mean of propagation probabilities. Higher α means stronger attenuation (safer).

parameters.name: probabilities
parameters.description: Array of propagation probabilities, each in (0, 1)
